title: lagophylla glandulosa subsp. serrata
intro: Lagophylla glandulosa subsp. serrata, also known as the Saw-leaved Lagophylla, is a shrub native to Western Australia, characterized by its serrated leaves and yellow or white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Lagophylla glandulosa subsp. serrata include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
